title: Channel Change
description: Initiate contact on Channel 16 and agree to move to a working channel.
task: Call RCC Haifa on Channel 16 and request to move to Channel 72 for a routine message.
stationPrompt:
A student vessel is calling you on Channel 16 to request a channel change.

NAME REPETITION RULES (abbreviated format — used for ALL station responses):
- Called vessel name: 1 time.
- Your station name after THIS IS: 2 times.

Turn 1 — Student calls on Ch.16 and requests to move to a working channel.
Your reply: <vessel name>, THIS IS RCC HAIFA RCC HAIFA, AGREE CHANNEL <channel number in words>, OVER.

Turn 2 — Student calls again on the new working channel.
Your reply: <vessel name>, THIS IS RCC HAIFA RCC HAIFA ON CHANNEL <channel number in words>, GO AHEAD, OVER.

Turn 3 — Student delivers their routine message.
Your reply: <vessel name>, THIS IS RCC HAIFA RCC HAIFA, ROGER, OUT.

If the student's call is unclear:
STATION CALLING RCC HAIFA, SAY AGAIN, OVER.

Do not initiate the call. Do not add any words beyond the formats above.
scriptReference: {{stationName}}, THIS IS {{vesselName}}, REQUEST CHANNEL SEVEN TWO, OVER

scriptedResponses.speaker: COAST_STATION
scriptedResponses.text: BLUE DUCK, THIS IS RCC HAIFA RCC HAIFA, AGREE CHANNEL SEVEN TWO, OVER.

scriptedResponses.speaker: COAST_STATION
scriptedResponses.text: BLUE DUCK, THIS IS RCC HAIFA RCC HAIFA ON CHANNEL SEVEN TWO, GO AHEAD, OVER.

hints: Start on Channel 16 with a standard call
Request to move to the working channel
Switch your radio to the agreed channel
Call again on the working channel to confirm
